page: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine instance belonging to the BS family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.6 GHz. The hardware configuration provides 2 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core, alongside 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX512F, and AES. Local storage is limited to an 8 GB temporary disk, though the instance is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments. No dedicated GPU resources are included.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the B2s is positioned at the lower end of the compute spectrum. Synthetic CPU evaluations via stress-ng and Geekbench place both single-core and multi-core performance in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% categories, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show weak throughput, with large block read-only speeds at 22.46 GB/sec and write speeds at 18.99 GB/sec. The burstable CPU allocation model means that the instance relies on a credit system to handle CPU spikes, making sustained high-utilization workloads inefficient. However, it demonstrates average performance in lightweight tasks, such as text generation using a small 135M parameter language model, which achieved 92.32 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the B2s offers a budget-conscious profile designed for resource-light environments. Because users pay a lower baseline rate and accumulate CPU credits for burst periods, it represents an economical choice for workloads that remain idle or run at low average CPU utilization. This resource density and pricing model make the B2s suitable for low-traffic web servers, small databases, development and testing environments, microservices, and basic background task processing.
description: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine from the BS family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 2 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 8 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, making it highly cost-effective for workloads with variable CPU demands. Benchmark results generally place its compute and memory performance in the lower percentiles, with weak single-core and multi-core scores. Consequently, the B2s is best suited for low-intensity applications, such as small-scale web serving, development and testing environments, lightweight database operations, and microservices that do not require sustained high CPU performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor, offering a budget-friendly profile for low-intensity workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B2s is a burstable x86_64 virtual machine with 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 8 GB local storage, designed for low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, burstable virtual machine with balanced memory and compute resources for lightweight, non-sustained development and testing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor at 2.6 GHz
Features 2 vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 8 GB local storage
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model for cost efficiency
Premium Storage capable for flexible external disk options
Best suited for low-traffic web servers, microservices, and testing
